blocks.content: Eli found out Monday. He was halfway through the bike rack speech about something unrelated when he stopped mid-sentence.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: With Daisy. Something happened with Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You have a face right now.
Eli put both hands on his bike and stared at the ground for a full three seconds.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We talked. About the song. About — things that had happened. A lot of things.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I'm making it exactly as weird as it deserves to be. You have been not-saying this thing since we were in middle school.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We still don't have a plan for August.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You have until August. Figure it out then.

Kit unlocked his bike. He didn't say anything else. But Eli was right, and he knew it, and for the first time in a long time the August problem felt like something that could be figured out rather than something to avoid looking at.
